leads.profitability_check: PASS - profitable in 2025; uranium segment earnings strong on higher realized prices and Westinghouse equity contribution

evidence: Signed a long-term agreement to supply uranium ore concentrate (U3O8) to the Government of India's Department of Atomic Energy for its fleet of nuclear reactors
Contract covers nearly 22 million pounds of U3O8 over nine years on market-related price terms, total value estimated at approximately USD 2.6 billion; deliveries expected 2027 through 2035
Cameco cites India's 24 operating reactors and plans to reach 100 GW nuclear by 2047 as demand driver; volumes were folded into the long-term contracting disclosure in the 2025 annual MD&A (Feb 2026)
Context: India's 2025 nuclear-liability/civil-nuclear liberalisation (SHANTI Act) and cooling India-Canada diplomatic tensions directly enable this deal
india_apac_interest.india_operations: No India assets; direct long-term supply relationship with India's Department of Atomic Energy (deliveries from 2027)
india_apac_interest.report_used: Cameco 2025 annual MD&A (Feb 2026) + SEC Form 6-K/40-F exhibits (sec.gov, full-text blocked 403; details via filing summaries)

leads.profitability_check: PASS - Potash adjusted EBITDA USD 2.25B in 2025 on record sales volumes; group profitable

evidence: India represents 6% of Canpotex sales volumes (Canpotex is Nutrien's offshore potash marketing arm)

2025 record offshore potash volumes explicitly supported by consumption growth in key offshore markets incl. India; relevant to India's fertilizer-security push (NIPU-2026 context)

india_apac_interest.report_used: Nutrien 2025 Form 40-F / annual report and 6-K market-outlook exhibits (sec.gov)

Retains long-standing India manufacturing footprint (e.g., Sanand, Gujarat: 215,000 sq ft seating plant and 356,000 sq ft body & chassis plant announced for multiple customers) plus large India engineering/R&D centers

CAE Simulation Training Pvt Ltd (CSTPL), 50:50 JV with InterGlobe Enterprises: 16 full-flight simulators at fiscal-end with 112% capacity utilization in FY2025 (two simulators added in-year)

leads.sector: Specialty Steel & Metals (iron ore, nickel, copper)

evidence: Vale executive vice president publicly stated Vale 'intends to expand its business in India', seeing a great opportunity to boost iron ore sales as 'India is emerging as a new hub for growth in the steel sector' (Kallanish)

WEG Motors Managing Director: the new unit will facilitate 'penetration of the low-voltage motor market in India and expand its participation in the local market with new opportunities'; WEG also joined Brazil business missions to India

leads.ticker: BOVESPA:EMBJ3 (per candidate CSV; common line is EMBR3)

evidence: Oct 2025: Embraer Defense & Security and Mahindra Group signed a Strategic Cooperation Agreement for the C-390 Millennium in the Indian Air Force's Medium Transport Aircraft (MTA) programme, deepening a Feb 2024 MoU - covering joint marketing, industrialisation and 'developing India as a hub for the C-390 Millennium'
Long-term ambition stated: position India as a manufacturing and support hub for the C-390 'serving both domestic and regional requirements' (local manufacturing, assembly, supply chain, MRO)

Feb 2026: Embraer-Mahindra announced planned C-390 MRO capability in India (base/heavy maintenance, component repair, avionics, training); June 2026 (FlightGlobal): Embraer prepared to open an Indian C-390 assembly line as part of the MTA bid

leads.sector: Green Energy & Fuels (thermal coal exporter; India = key market)

evidence: India is described as a cornerstone export market for Thungela, 'absorbing large volumes amid rising power needs and limited domestic supply'; exports go to India, Asia, Southeast Asia, Middle East and North Africa

evidence: SABIC Integrated Annual Report 2025 and corporate disclosures: invested more than USD 100 million in the SABIC Technology & Innovation Center in Bengaluru - described as one of the largest investments in India by a Saudi company - with 300+ research scientists and engineers 'driving innovation... for India and for the world'
Corporate positioning statement: 'SABIC in India, for the World' - excited to partner in India's on-going growth journey (sabic.com news)

evidence: July 2025 (Riyadh, witnessed by Union Minister J.P. Nadda and the Saudi Minister of Industry and Mineral Resources): Ma'aden signed long-term agreements with Indian companies IPL, KRIBHCO and CIL for supply of an enhanced 3.1 million MT of DAP fertilizer per annum for five years from FY2025-26, extendable five more years by mutual consent

Argus: India offtake covers 3.1 Mt/yr of phosphates over five years from India's 2025-26 fiscal year, mostly DAP plus NPS - agreed as India scrambles to replace vanished Chinese DAP exports

20-F also discloses 26% holding in HALBIT Avionics Pvt Ltd (Bangalore), engaged in avionics programs for the Indian defense market

Elbit describes itself as a trusted partner of the Indian MoD/Armed Forces (~70-year legacy); regular Aero India participation; India military EO/IR market (where Elbit is a leading player) projected USD 1.19bn (2026) to 1.78bn (2030)
india_apac_interest.india_operations: Adani-Elbit Hyderabad UAV manufacturing JV (49%); HALBIT Avionics Bangalore (26%); ongoing Indian armed-forces programs (UAVs, EO/IR, artillery upgrades)

leads.profitability_check: PASS - profitable; Q2 sales USD 1.8B with specialty segments driving growth

evidence: June 2026 (6-K): new agreement with Indian Potash Limited (IPL) to supply 375,000 MT of potash over the coming year (option for 50,000 extra tons) at prevailing Indian market prices, within ICL's 2022-2027 five-year supply framework with IPL - 'reinforces its strategic position in India's fertilizer market'
Long-term Polysulphate agreement with IPL: five-year term for an aggregate 1 million MT through 2026 with renewal option
Annual results attribute higher potash volumes 'mainly due to higher sales volumes in India and Europe'; India is a recurring named market in ICL's annual 20-F potash segment

method_notes: Candidate pool verified: all 14 selected names grep-confirmed present in candidates_ca_br_za_sa.csv (1,838 rows). Requested names NOT in the pool and therefore excluded: Sibanye-Stillwater, Teva, Aramco parent (only Aramco-adjacent Luberef SASE:2223 present). South Africa pharma slot: Aspen Pharmacare (JSE:APN) is in the pool but was dropped after searches found no concrete India operations/plans in its FY2025 disclosures; replaced with Thungela Resources (JSE:TGA, in-pool) which has a strong, named India export dependence.

Country split: Canada 5 (Cameco, Nutrien, Magna, CAE, Teck), Brazil 3 (Vale, WEG, Embraer), South Africa 2 (Sasol, Thungela), Saudi Arabia 2 (SABIC, Ma'aden), Israel 2 (Elbit, ICL). Bombardier (in-pool) dropped: post rail-divestiture business-jet focus has thin India relevance.

~26 web searches/fetches performed against SEDAR/SEC filings (40-F, 20-F, 6-K), company IR PDFs (Sasol AFS FY2025, SABIC IAR 2025, Teck 2025 AR, CAE FY2025, Magna 2025 AR) and corroborating trade press (Argus, Kallanish, FlightGlobal, Mining Weekly).

Policy context captured: India's civil-nuclear liberalisation (SHANTI Act 2025 / liability reform) is the direct enabler of the Cameco-DAE USD 2.6B deal; India-Canada diplomatic thaw (2025) underpins renewed Canadian resource deals (Cameco uranium, Canpotex potash); India-Saudi fertilizer axis (Ma'aden 3.1 Mt/yr DAP, SABIC Agri-Nutrients) is partly a response to China's DAP export withdrawal; India-UAE CEPA was reviewed but is not load-bearing for any Gulf lead here (Saudi leads route through bilateral India-Saudi ministerial agreements, not CEPA).

Profitability flags are honest: SABIC posted a FY2025 reported net LOSS (divestiture fair-value charges; adjusted income positive) and Sasol only returned to profit in FY2025 with continued impairments and no dividend - both flagged rather than filtered out because their India relevance is structural.

Ticker caveat: candidate CSV lists Embraer as BOVESPA:EMBJ3; the liquid common line is EMBR3 (yfinance EMBR3.SA, NYSE ADR ERJ). Teck B-shares on yfinance are TECK-B.TO. Elbit and ICL are dual-listed (TASE/US); yfinance alternatives: ESLT (NASDAQ), ICL.TA.
site_access_issues: sec.gov full-text exhibit fetch returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (Cameco 6-K); deal terms cross-verified via multiple secondary sources (StockTitan filing summary, Rutland Herald wire copy) instead of the raw exhibit
Magna 2025 annual report PDF (magna.com) not parsed page-by-page; India divestiture figure (-USD 112M sales) comes from annual-report-derived search snippets - page-level quote UNVERIFIED
WEG integrated annual report 2025 India section not directly quotable; India evidence taken from WEG's own institutional news releases and Indian registry financials (Tofler/Tracxn)
Tadawul filings for SABIC/Ma'aden accessed via company English IR sites and wire coverage rather than tadawul.com.sa directly
Aspen Pharmacare FY2025 disclosures contain no findable India-specific operations statement (searches of aspenpharma.com and results booklets) - reason for exclusion, recorded here for transparency
